title: Burden of disease
subtitle: [Disability-Adjusted Life Years](#dod:dalys) (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.
caption: In 2021, the global burden of disease, measured in Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals, displays a distinct geographic distribution. The map uses a color scale ranging from light beige for the lowest values to deep maroon for the highest, revealing that the most severe disease burdens cluster across sub-Saharan Africa. Countries in this region, including the Central African Republic, Chad, and Somalia, appear in the darkest shades, corresponding to the highest end of the scale above 40,000 DALYs. In contrast, North America, Western Europe, and Australia show lighter orange tones, indicating lower burdens generally falling between 10,000 and 20,000 DALYs. Russia and parts of Eastern Europe stand out with darker red coloring compared to their western neighbors, while Greenland and parts of the Sahara are marked as having no data. The visualization shows a clear gradient where the highest disease burden per capita concentrates in the central and western African belt.
